sentences.text: Hey, good morning, guys.
Good morning.
How we feeling?
Good, how are you?
Good, still pretty tired, but I think maybe by tomorrow I'll be over it.
Yes.
I'm going to Vegas, so should be good recovery.
Yeah, that's just what the doctor ordered.
Known for its rest and relaxation.
Yeah, and then hiking… hiking the Narrows after that, so….
Oh my god.
Yeah.
She did.

Mitch, I know I sent you a little slack about this meeting, but basically, they've been asking about data delivery options, historical data delivery options, so I'm thinking here we just want to understand what the scope of what the delivery they'd like is.
And then, Matt, I guess we… like, I don't know how we price that, because they're actually on paper for the next year.
It's like, we just have to take, … we just have to have takeaways for this, like, the API thing is coming up more and more.
like, it's a priority after Explore Premium is out, so it sounds like we're probably, like, 2 months out from any deliverable on this.
door.
pricing on it.
So, I'd say for today, we're just trying to understand scope.
what they're looking for, and then summarize it, and then get it over to Megan and Alec as another API request that we received.
Alright, cool, I'm gonna let him in.
Yep.
Any questions missed before I do so, or you're good?
No, we'll just, you know, typical discovery stuff, so….
Awesome.
Good morning, everybody.
Good morning.
How we feeling, Joe?
How's Friday going?
It's Friday.
Happy Friday.
Happy Friday!
How is your, … was it you were traveling earlier this week, or was it, … I forgot, did you have something going on earlier this week?
I did, I was, I was at, I was at the RealPage User Conference.
Alright, how was that?
out in Vegas.
Hot.
Yeah.
It was, it was good.
… So, yeah, I… I may have told you this, may not have told you, so I used to work for RealPage, and … it was strange.
I haven't been in a couple years, and I hardly knew anybody.
It was… it was weird.
I used to go to those, I'd know, like.
you know, 100 people there I'd know, I knew….
you're the mayor, right?
I knew, like, 10 people at the whole conference.
Wow.
And, like, 9 of them were former RealPage colleagues.
But it was good, I mean, there was some good stuff there, so, you know, it wasn't a washout or anything, but it was just strange.
Yeah.
We just… we just had our company off-site, Joe, and as you know, we've been… been growing pretty quickly, so….
Yeah.
It was, like, similar… so, like, percentage-wise, the number of people you are meeting for the first time are, you know, like, who is… who is that person?
And so….
You guys are 100% remote, right?
100% remote, so, yeah.

It's like you get… if you get lucky if you start, like, kinda right before one of our offsites, otherwise, you know, our sales gets out quite a bit, but, like, if you're a developer or something, you know, you're… you only most, you know developers from your quarterly meetings and things like that, so….
Sure.
But….
When are you guys gonna have a user conference?
Oh, I don't know.
We've been doing, like, the leadership conference circuit this year, like a lot of those trade shows and stuff, but I think we're probably getting close to that point.
I think we're probably getting… I think we're well-liked in the industry, and … we usually have enough new product to talk about, so it probably makes sense to do it at some point.
Yeah, I would think so.
Yeah.
Where should we have it, Joe?
Where would you have it?
Not Vegas in the summertime.
I'm going to Vegas tomorrow, by the way.
You're going tomorrow?
Yes.
Oh, so, I'm sure you'll have fun.
Just try to stay indoors as much as possible.
It was literally 109 degrees there on Tuesday.
Yeesh.
Yeah, it's fun.
I've been… I'm gonna be in Vegas 4 or 5 times this year, so….
That is….
Dude, your friends are gonna start worrying about you.
Four or five Vegas trips in a year, that's… I think more than two is concern, right?
Yeah.
5 more times than I would normally go.

It's cool to go there, like… for a huge sporting event, like the NCAA tournament, or the NFL playoffs, or NBA playoffs, something like that, and go to a sportsbook with your friends, and… and… that's a fun time.
That's probably the best time I ever had there, was doing that.
Yeah, this is, this is for personal.
This is, my significant other's mother is getting remarried next Monday.
Okay.
And, back in March, when it was Sierra's birthday, I was pretty late on getting a birthday gift, so I deferred to….
Backstreet Boys tickets at the Sphere.
Okay.
out on the West Coast, so I was like, let's make a week out of it, but….
Yeah, big save.
Big save.
Yeah.
Yeah, well, now it's time to pay the piper, so… I'll get there tomorrow.
Yeah.
Awesome.
Well, thanks for getting back together, Joe.
I know we kind of met on this a week or two ago, and in complete transparency, like, we would love to just leave today getting kind of a scope of what you all are looking for.
Sure.
Our development team right now is on the revenue management tool and the Explorer Premium that I think we've talked through, but APIs are next up on that.
We do have the data sync options to get you some of this data just via that endpoint.
Sure.
But we're hoping today to just get some scope so we can start to put some pricing around, let's discuss how we want to move forward, so….
So, I see Subro on the call.
Hey, Subro, how you doing?
Hey, I'm doing good, thank you very much.
For her, you know?
The last time, Easter time, I hope, Everybody's okay with that.
So, Subaru is the gatekeeper of our internal BI solutions, so he's the person you guys want to talk to when it comes to, you know, anything data-related.
Awesome.
And I guess this is probably just a good starting point, like, I know that the interest here is in historical data, and potentially data ongoing.
And so, I know in these conversations, more data is always better, but maybe just how you all are thinking about, what data points are important to you.
We have, like, over 100 data points, so what data points are most important, and at what scale?
But….
You're most interested in.
Okay.
So, you know, from the front end, I can see a lot of data points, KPIs, and all those metrics and everything in there.
what we do currently is that, you know, we have a data file that comes to us every day, through, you know, an API call or, you know, some call.

It's just one, data file which gives us all the properties, all the market areas, Is, … number of rooms and bedrooms and bathrooms types, and the rents information, along with some more information in there.
I'll show you that file as well, okay?
And that daily data, that's the only file that we store into a table, okay?
And we started getting that file since December sometimes, November, December time sometimes, right?
Now, what we are looking to do is that, you know, how can we put a trend from the last, you know, 5 years, 4 years, before COVID time, and see how this thing's changing?
And I see the data is available from 2023 onwards in ApartmentIQ, right?
So we're just wondering, can we get that data, set, download from at least one time?
If not, there's a call to get the data?
So I was exploring, after I got the ApartmentIQ access, and I was exploring that and tried to see that.
So, if you can help us to get to that.
Then we can download that, dataset, and then we can, you know, one time, upload into our system, okay, for analytics purpose.
Got it.
So….
Phil, maybe you can remind me on this, because I know I came into the RMR relationship midway through, so Data Sync is currently enabled?
I believe we have Data Sync enabled, yeah.
So you're receiving… right now, you're receiving the property level, floor plan level, unit level data on a daily basis.
Yeah.
for the properties that RMR is tracking.
Yeah.
And you're just looking for that same scope of data.
In scope of data.
Going back to, I think, I believe it's October of 2021, or October of 2020.
Yeah.
Okay.
So if I can share and show those things, maybe it'll be a little easier.

Yeah, I think I have an understanding, but let's, just for clarity purposes, yeah, if you'd like to, and then… I think the answer to this is yes, we can do that, and … it's more of a conversation around…
How we all would think about pricing that.
Okay.
All right, so I don't know what is going in there, but anyway.
You can see my screen, right?
Yes.
Okay.
So, this is the dataset that we get every day, okay?
This comes, you know… it is an old sample, but it's the same thing.
about, all these datasets for the market, market ID, and a whole lot of things.
I just put… pivoted this into this one.
… So, what we are looking into is this, of course, you know, all these data points comes in from here.
They're lat-long, … Data, tracking started.
Unit counts, air builds.
Days on the market.
Important is this, you know, beds and baths and the rent information.
Along with the square footage.
So that's what we are, looking for, this, these four, you know.
These metrics, basically, with all this.
Dimensions?
This is the data file that we… we received, okay?
Yep.
Okay.
I was trying to, you know, poke around with the reports and stuff in ApartmentIQ.
Okay?
So I see there are, … The reports that are available, which, goes back, you know, data set.
And I have been doing for… on a daily level, so it goes back up to 2023.
That's good enough for us, okay?
And I was doing this into… and I can export these CSV files for this, but I'm not sure if I'm getting all the KPIs into these reports?
And certainly, I have to do one by one property, then by bedroom types and all those things, by floor plan, by units, so I have to run it several times to do the same kind of report.
That's what I was thinking, that is it possible?
To get the signal dataset, … As of now, you know, historical data set up from W-23, so that's… the ask.
It is, and it's actually possible to go further back than that.
One question that I would have there is, are gross or net effective rents important to you?
I know.
ideally both.
The reason that I ask, maybe, Joe, you can jump in here, is the concession data in IQ, only goes back about 2 years.
Okay, that's… that's fine.
Okay.
Gross rents will go back further, to 2021, but it's effectively the same data files.
That can go all the way back.
… the… and I'll have to think on this, Joan, maybe get with finance.
Typically, how we do this with a partner is they have, data sync contracted in.
And then it's a incremental, like, per unit cost on that data sync to get the historical.
And we ask for a two-year contract on it, because once you have the four years of historical data, it's a one-time drop, and you have it, if that makes sense.
Yep.
So, … I think I understand this.
I probably made this more complicated than it needed to be.
I thought we were thinking, like, broader MSA data, but if you're just looking for the set of data you're getting now back further, not a problem.
We just have to figure out how to price that and make sure that we get to a place that works for both parties.
Okay.
Well, that was easy.
I was gonna say, we, I mean….
You know?
This is an expensive meeting from both sides, so….
Right?
Friday last time, don't want to extend more, but I have one more quick question.
Yeah, yeah, of course, of course.
So, I saw that, you know, I can create some reports, schedule some reports on my own.
And, I… And I created, this morning one of the reports where I have some limitations that I can only add 10 of these markets, not more than that, this one I created.
Although we have more than that.
So, is there a limitation?
And also, I can get only a PDF file.
But I wanted an Excel file, if possible.
Mitch, are you still… yeah, can you speak to this?

Yeah, yeah, Subro, that's unfortunately just a limitation in the front-end experience, so it's not like a hard cap, I think you just, from this page that you're on, where it says schedule report, … It's just, we limit the number that you can input here, but if you created a new scheduled report, and kind of, like, you could even have it delivered on the same date to the same recipients.
And then do the other 10.
It's just a cap on, you know, it's a form field limit here.
We're actually working on rebuilding the entire scheduled reports page to make it a lot easier for you to use.
So unfortunately, that's just one of the limitations in the front end that we have.
Okay, and the other one is this PDF file.
I saw there's a possibility of creating an Excel file, I didn't see that option.
When I was creating that for myself.
Yeah, yeah, so, how many… are you hoping to do this for your entire portfolio?
Yeah.
Okay.

So the way… I'll explain the workaround to do it, but if this is something that you want help with, our support team would be more than happy to just, configure this for you so that you don't have to click through so much.
The way I would recommend doing it right now is if you click to any one of your market surveys.
And open that one up.
here?
Yep.
Okay.
And then if you hit export in the top right.
Oh, okay.
And then Excel will be the one?
And then if you select that, you'll be able to schedule that.
Of course, the drawback is that this is one market survey at a time.
So, that's time-consuming, so we'd be more than happy to configure that on our end.
That is kind of the part of the experience that we're working on improving, so that it's easier to bulk schedule your reports moving forward.
Got it.
Yeah.
Yeah, that's not super urgent, but I was just wondering that, you know, why can I not get all the properties at a time?
Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.

That's, yeah, just on our side, the, the scheduled reports page was built actually before we even had Excel reports, and so that's where there's just, like, a little bit of a gap, and we're investing in, Closing that and revamping it.
Sounds good.
Okay, I'll work with finance.
I, I suppose I'll present two options, one that goes back to, 2023, which would be inclusive of NER, and then one that goes back further.
And then, depending on what you want to budget for, how far back you need, we can continue the conversation from there.
Sounds good.
Sounds good.
I think….
Alex, … was there.
Do you have any questions?
On the data, range of data.
Nope.
Just a quick question, so the earliest data you have is 2019, is that right?
October 2020, I believe, is the start.
Okay.
Caveat of that is, at that point, we were tracking around 10 million units, and we're now around, 37 million units, I believe, so….
Okay.
Some of that is just natural, like, development or new supply, some of that is us getting much better at, understanding….
Different property management softwares and how to pull the data out.
Understood, thank you.
Yep.
And then, Joe, I believe we're, like, mid-contract, so the way I'm thinking about this is maybe, like, a single-time cost in a, like, a additional, like, extension on that.
But I'll… we can, talk offline on that.
Sure.
Okay.
All right, I guess only 10 minutes back, but, good to see you all, and, and, ….
Good seeing you guys.
Have a wonderful weekend.
Thanks, you too, appreciate your time, everyone.
Take care.
Bye, guys.

conversation.title: RMR <> Apt IQ; Historic Data Touchbase
